- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- _sk-read-big-file: Read large files in manageable chunks when they exceed Claude's context limits. Use when working with large log files, data files, or when you get token limit errors reading files.
- _sk-cross-platform-sh: Review shell scripts for macOS + Linux compatibility. Use when the user asks to check, audit, or fix cross-platform issues in .sh files. Catches BSD vs GNU differences and bash version pitfalls.
- _sk-linear: Fetch Linear issue details and save to repo
- _sk-video-analyzer: Extract screenshots and transcribe audio from any video file
- _sk-branch-ctx: Manage branch-ctx (bctx) - git branch context manager. Use when user asks about branch contexts, _branch/ folder, .bctx/ config, or bctx commands. Auto-invoke when repo has .bctx/ directory.
- _sk-penpot: Create and edit designs in Penpot via MCP. Use when user asks to create mockups, wireframes, UI designs, or interact with Penpot files.
- _sk-devpanel: Manage DevPanel VSCode extension config - add tasks, variables, replacements, git excludes. Use when user asks to configure DevPanel or add items to .devpanel/config.jsonc.
- _sk-sheet: Control Google Sheets - read/write cells, manage sheets, import/export data. Use when user asks to interact with spreadsheets.
- _sk-afk: Enable AFK mode - registers session for Telegram watch, enables remote communication via HTTP API. Use when user is going AFK and wants to keep interacting via Telegram.
- _sk-telegram-message: Send Telegram messages to the user via Bot API. Use when Claude Code needs to notify the user about task completion, errors, or any important event.
- _sk-chrome: Control Chrome browser - take screenshots, extract HTML, click elements, execute JS, get console logs. Use when user asks to interact with browser tabs.
- _sk-loom-download: Download Loom videos to local filesystem

## Vitest Testing Framework Overview

Vitest is a next generation testing framework powered by Vite. Here are the key aspects:

### What It Is
Vitest was created to make testing just work for Vite apps. By building on top of Vite, Vitest natively understands your Vite config and is able to reuse the same resolve and transform pipelines.

### Key Features
- **Performance**: Vitest leverages Vite as its foundation, utilizing its lightning-fast hot module replacement (HMR) and esbuild for bundling and transpilation. Tests are re-run only for affected files, making the feedback loop instantaneous.

- **Modern JavaScript Support**: Vitest has first-class support for modern JavaScript out of the box: it handles ESM, TypeScript, and JSX natively via Vite's transformer (powered by Oxc).

- **Jest Compatibility**: Vitest provides a Jest-compatible API, so you can continue to use familiar testing libraries without extra setup.

- **Built-in Tools**: Vitest offers native code coverage via v8 or istanbul, and Jest-compatible mocking, stubbing, and spies.

### Setup
Vitest reads your vite.config.* by default, so your existing Vite plugins and configuration work out-of-the-box.

### Adoption
There are 1706 other projects in the npm registry using vitest.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Vitest is a next-generation testing framework powered by Vite. It offers Jest-compatible API, native ESM/TypeScript/JSX support, built-in code coverage, and fast HMR-based test re-runs.
